title: Add Manual Trade Monitoring with Entry Point Tracking

requirements.text: Create a new Trade Monitoring view accessible from the main navigation tabs that allows users to manually input trade details including: asset symbol (e.g., BTCUSDT), leverage used (1x-125x), position direction (Long or Short), and entry price point.

quotes: adicionar uma opção.. de monitoramento... escolhendo o ativo e colocando o ponto de entrada.. manualmente
O usuário informa o ativo. A alavancagem usada. Se foi long ou short.

acceptanceCriteria: New tab/view added to main navigation for Trade Monitoring
Form accepts asset symbol with validation against Binance Futures USDT pairs
Leverage selector supports range from 1x to 125x
Direction toggle for Long/Short positions
Entry price input field with numeric validation
Form validation prevents submission with incomplete data

requirements.text: After user submits trade details, display real-time monitoring panel showing current price, unrealized PnL percentage based on entry point and direction, and liquidation price calculated using the specified leverage.
acceptanceCriteria: Panel displays current market price fetched from Binance
PnL calculation is accurate for both Long and Short positions
Liquidation price calculated correctly based on leverage (formula: Long liquidation = entry * (1 - 1/leverage), Short liquidation = entry * (1 + 1/leverage))
Real-time price updates every 3-5 seconds
Visual indicators (colors) change based on profit/loss status

requirements.text: Generate and display suggested price targets based on current market data and technical analysis: show 3 take-profit zones (TP1 at 1-2% from entry, TP2 at 3-5%, TP3 at 5-10%) adjusted for position direction, with percentage gains calculated for each zone.

quotes: E o aplicativo... indica.. direções..  possiveis. Areas de realização.

acceptanceCriteria: Display 3 distinct take-profit zones (TP1, TP2, TP3)
TP calculations respect position direction (above entry for Long, below for Short)
Show both absolute price and percentage gain for each TP zone
TP zones adjust based on asset volatility using ATR or recent price movement data
Visual markers indicate current price position relative to TP zones

requirements.text: Calculate and display recommended stop-loss price based on risk management principles: suggest stop-loss at 1-2% below entry for Long positions (above for Short), with adjustments based on asset volatility and support/resistance levels from technical analysis.
acceptanceCriteria: Stop-loss recommendation displayed with absolute price and percentage distance from entry
Direction-aware calculation (below entry for Long, above for Short)
Base stop-loss at 1-2% adjusted for high/low volatility assets
Consider nearby support/resistance zones from technical analysis when available
Show risk-reward ratio (potential gain to TP zones vs stop-loss risk)

requirements.text: Allow users to save and track multiple monitored trades simultaneously with persistent storage, showing a list of active positions with quick summary cards displaying symbol, direction, entry, current PnL, and time elapsed since entry.
acceptanceCriteria: Users can save multiple trade setups without limit
Trades persist in localStorage or IndexedDB across sessions
List view shows all active monitored trades with summary information
Each trade card displays: symbol, long/short badge, entry price, current PnL, elapsed time
Users can select a trade to view full monitoring details
Option to close/remove completed trades from monitoring list

requirements.text: Integrate the Trade Monitoring feature with existing market data hooks (useQueries) to fetch real-time price updates from Binance Futures API directly in the frontend, maintaining consistency with the established frontend-only data fetching pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: Reuses existing Binance Futures USD-M data fetching logic
No backend HTTP calls required (frontend-only implementation)
Price updates leverage React Query caching and refetch policies
Error handling for Binance API blocks/restrictions
Graceful degradation when market data is unavailable

constraints: All Binance API calls must be made directly from the frontend browser - backend cannot be used for external HTTP requests
Do not modify immutable frontend paths: useInternetIdentity.ts, useActor.ts, main.tsx, components/ui directory
Trade monitoring calculations (PnL, liquidation, stop-loss) must work without requiring API keys or authenticated Binance access
Use existing market data structures and hooks from useQueries.ts for consistency
nonGoals: Actual trade execution or order placement on Binance
Integration with Binance API keys for account-level data
Backend storage or processing of trade monitoring data
Real-time WebSocket connections for price streaming
Automated trade management or bot functionality
Portfolio performance analytics across multiple trades
